_readme: The cross-surface capability inventory. parity_test.go derives the real TUI,
web, and CLI surfaces from code and fails when any of them grows a capability
with no entry here. Adding a CLI verb, a daemon route, a TUI binding, or a web
RPC therefore forces a deliberate answer to 'what about the other two?'.
status: yes | no | partial | n/a   (n/a = the surface has no analogue by nature)
parity     - all applicable surfaces expose it
real-gap   - a surface should have it and does not; 'issue' names the ticket
deliberate - the surface legitimately cannot or should not; 'notes' says why,
so it is never re-reported as a gap
unclear    - needs an owner decision; do not file until triaged
Pointers are file:line into the surface's own code. Keep them accurate: they
are the difference between an actionable issue and noise.

capabilities.title: Create a session
capabilities.notes: The verb is at parity; the OPTIONS are not. See the session.create.opt.* rows — no surface is a superset of another.

capabilities.title: Set the new session's title
capabilities.notes: TUI/web send title_base so the daemon auto-suffixes collisions; the CLI sends title and errors on collision instead.

capabilities.title: Choose the agent program at create time
Send an initial prompt with the new session
capabilities.notes: Inverted gap: the TUI is the surface that lacks this. sessionStartRequest.Prompt exists and is plumbed to the daemon (app/session_control.go:106) but its only construction site never populates it, so the field is dead from the TUI.

capabilities.title: Set auto-yes for one session at create time

cli.pointer: api/sessions.go:326 sends cfg.AutoYes; no per-create flag (root -y is Flags(), not PersistentFlags())

capabilities.notes: The web is the ONLY surface with per-session auto-yes. TUI inherits the launch-time -y flag for every session in the process; the CLI reads the repo config and `af sessions create --autoyes` is an unknown-flag error. Deliberately NOT filed: this is a divergence in MODEL (process-wide vs per-session), and converging on the web's granularity presumes the web is the correct one — the checkbox may equally be the anomaly. Auto-yes is still flagged [experimental] (commands/root.go:251). Owner call.

capabilities.title: Choose the session's backend (local/docker/ssh/hook) per session

tui.pointer: app/session_control.go:88-95 sessionStartRequest has no Backend field

web.pointer: web/src/api.ts:251-264 sends 5 of the route's 9 fields; no backend

capabilities.notes: The CLI is the only surface that can pick a backend per session. Neither TUI nor web is hard-locked to local: both send backend:"", which falls through to the repo's in-repo `backend` config key (session/instance_factory.go:102-114), so in a repo configured backend=ssh their sessions ARE remote. What is missing is per-session CHOICE, not remote sessions outright.

capabilities.title: Force a session onto the remote hook backend
capabilities.notes: TUI is 'partial' because ForceRemote resolves to the hook backend ONLY (session/instance_factory.go:106-108) — N cannot reach docker or ssh. The web has no remote affordance at all; this is the gap the owner reported.

capabilities.title: Attach a session to the repo's existing working tree (--here)

tui.pointer: no InPlace assignment anywhere in app/ (grep-verified)

capabilities.notes: CLI-only. Plausibly deliberate: --here is a scripting affordance for 'run an agent right here in my checkout', and the TUI/web are worktree-oriented session managers. Needs an owner call before filing.

capabilities.title: List sessions
Get one session's record
capabilities.notes: A scripting read-verb. The TUI and web render the whole projection continuously, so 'get one record' has no UI analogue — the row is already on screen.

capabilities.title: Send a prompt to an existing session

tui.pointer: interactive mode types straight into the pane (app/interactive.go:147)

capabilities.notes: The TUI reaches this by typing into the live terminal rather than via the SendPrompt RPC; same user capability, different transport.

capabilities.title: Broadcast one prompt to many sessions (--all/--all-repos)
capabilities.notes: A fan-out scripting verb with no interactive analogue: broadcasting to every live session is a batch operation, and both UIs are single-selection by construction.

capabilities.title: Permanently destroy a session
Archive a session (restorable)
capabilities.notes: Reachable everywhere — but see session.restore: on the web this is a one-way door.

capabilities.title: Restore an archived, Lost, or Dead session

web.pointer: no RestoreSession/RestoreArchived call anywhere in web/src (grep-verified)

capabilities.notes: Highest-impact gap found. The web can archive but not restore, so archive is a reachable dead end. Worse, the web already handles the session.restored event (web/src/sessions.ts:85) and its own copy instructs users to do the thing it cannot do: modals.ts:261 'You can restore it later.', modals.ts:292 'restore any of them to bring the project back', split.ts:868 'Restore it to open VS Code.'

capabilities.title: Resume a session blocked at a usage-limit wall

web.pointer: renders the limit state (web/src/status.ts:57,137) but never calls ResumeFromLimit

cli.pointer: no limit/retry verb in the cobra tree (derived)

capabilities.notes: TUI-only. The route is served but parked in internalHTTPRoutes, and daemon/httproutes.go:185-188 admits this is provisional: 'ResumeFromLimit is a genuine client-facing session verb (the TUI `c` key); it lands here rather than the public catalog only to hold the catalog steady in this PR — promoting it to httpRoutes is a one-line follow-up.' That follow-up never happened. The web shows a session is limit-blocked with no way out.

capabilities.title: Attach to a session's terminal
Read a session's terminal content without attaching

tui.pointer: the rail renders live pane previews continuously

capabilities.notes: The CLI uses the internal Preview RPC; the UIs get the same content off the live stream.

capabilities.title: Preview a specific tab (by ordinal or stable id)

web.pointer: web/src/terminal.ts:205-223 streams any tab via tab_id

cli.pointer: api/sessions.go:674 sends only {Title, RepoID}; no --tab flag

capabilities.notes: The substrate supports it and one surface does not expose it: PreviewRequest carries Tab (ordinal) and TabID (stable id, #1738), the TUI drives both, and `af sessions preview <title>` can only ever see tab 0. Absurd in practice because the CLI can CREATE a tab (`af sessions tab-create --name x --command …`) and then has no way to read it — create without read. Found by dogfooding, not by the verb-level check, which is why field_coverage now exists.

capabilities.title: Preview a tab's full scrollback rather than the visible screen
capabilities.notes: PreviewRequest.Full selects PreviewFullHistory over the visible capture (daemon/preview.go:80 -> session/agentserver_local.go:122-130). `af sessions preview` can only ever show the visible screen, never scrollback — the same unexposed-field shape as session.preview-tab, on the same request, so it is tracked by the same issue.

capabilities.title: Choose a new tab's command, name, or kind at create time

web.pointer: web/src/api.ts:339,355 sets shell/kind but never command/name

cli.pointer: api/sessions_tabs.go:112 sends Command, Name, Kind, URL, Port

capabilities.notes: The TUI's `t` always spawns a bare $SHELL: it cannot name a tab or run a command. Overlaps the open tab-UX work in #1813 (tabs labelled 'Tab N', no rename), so it wants triaging with that rather than a fresh issue. Distinct from session.tab.create.web, which is a deliberate omission.

capabilities.title: Spawn a bare $SHELL tab with no command

cli.pointer: api/sessions_tabs.go:98-100 requires --command; no --shell flag, never sets Shell

capabilities.notes: Both UIs open a shell tab in one gesture; the CLI must be told a command. `--command $SHELL` is a plausible workaround but is NOT verified equivalent — Shell:true is a distinct wire field the daemon may treat differently (naming, kind resolution). Needs an owner call, and checking that equivalence, before filing.

capabilities.title: Address a session by its stable id rather than by title
capabilities.notes: Documented at daemon/control_types.go:226-230 and :48-54: 'TUI/CLI callers omit it and resolve by {Title, RepoID}'. The web sends the stable id because it is an all-repos client where a duplicate title across repos could target the wrong session; the TUI and CLI are repo-scoped (--repo / the focused project) so title resolution is unambiguous for them. The ID field being unset by the Go surfaces is by design, not drift.

capabilities.title: Block until a session goes idle
capabilities.notes: A blocking scripting primitive for automation. Both UIs show liveness continuously, so 'block until idle' is meaningless there — you just look.

capabilities.title: Identify the current session from inside it
capabilities.notes: Only meaningful to an agent running INSIDE a session's shell. Neither UI runs inside a session.

capabilities.title: Spawn a shell/process tab in a session
capabilities.notes: All three gate off-box backends: the web hides + / x for docker/ssh/remote (web/src/ui.ts:187-210) mirroring the daemon's Capabilities().TabManagement, and the TUI refuses at app/handle_actions.go:830-832.

capabilities.title: Spawn a VS Code tab

tui.pointer: no kind selector on the t binding

capabilities.notes: The TUI's t always spawns $SHELL. A VS Code tab is an editor window the TUI cannot render inline, so a TUI affordance may be pointless — but the tab would still exist for web/CLI consumers. Needs an owner call.

capabilities.title: Spawn a web tab (URL/port iframe)
capabilities.notes: Documented in code: 'A web tab is deliberately absent: its target comes from whatever an agent is running, so it stays CLI/API-created' (web/src/ui.ts:41-43, docs/web.md). Both UIs RENDER existing web tabs; only creation is CLI/API-scoped.

capabilities.title: Close a tab
capabilities.notes: The agent tab (index 0) is refused on every surface.

capabilities.title: Record a session's GitHub PR info

tui.pointer: app/session_control.go:308 (auto-fetched on sync, not a user verb)

cli.pointer: no PR verb in the cobra tree (derived)

capabilities.notes: Public route, but the TUI's use is background sync (it fetches PR info from git and records it) rather than a user action. Whether the web should also sync PR info — or whether this should be daemon-side for every client — is an owner call, not a filed gap.

capabilities.title: Open / copy a session's PR URL
capabilities.notes: Depends on session.pr.set: no other surface records PR info, so there is nothing for them to open. Triage together.

capabilities.title: See the list of projects
capabilities.notes: `af projects list` does not exist; projects are derivable from `af sessions list`. Low impact, but the group looks lopsided with a delete-only noun.

capabilities.title: Delete a project (archive its sessions, drop the grouping)
Register a new project

web.pointer: web/src/modals.ts:153-157 'No projects yet — create a session in the TUI first'

cli.pointer: a project is implicitly registered by `af sessions create --repo`

capabilities.notes: The web's own empty state tells users to go to the TUI, which is an explicit parity admission in copy. But projects are implicit (any repo you create a session in), so 'add project' may not warrant a first-class verb. Owner call.

capabilities.title: Scope the view to one project
capabilities.notes: The CLI expresses this as per-command --repo scoping rather than sticky state.

capabilities.title: List tasks
Add a task
Edit an existing task's name/prompt/trigger/target/program

web.pointer: web/src/index.ts:854-865 sends only {enabled}; no edit form exists

capabilities.notes: The web can create a task but never change it: the only UpdateTask call site is the enable/disable toggle. Its TS TaskUpdate type (web/src/types.ts:174-182) also omits project_path, so that field is unreachable from the web even in principle. To fix a typo'd cron the user must delete and re-add.

capabilities.title: Enable / disable a task
Remove a task
Trigger a task now
capabilities.notes: All three refuse watch-backed tasks; the web additionally hides the button (web/src/tasks.ts:92-94).

capabilities.title: Get one task's record
capabilities.notes: Scripting read-verb; both UIs render the task list continuously.

capabilities.title: Read global config
capabilities.notes: Config is a file the user edits. Whether the UIs should expose a settings view is a product question, not a drift bug — flagging rather than filing.

capabilities.title: Set a global config key
capabilities.notes: See config.read. Note the in-repo `backend`/docker/ssh keys are NOT settable from any surface (config/resolve.go:108-112) — they are checked-in repo config by design.

capabilities.title: Edit a repo's post-worktree hooks
capabilities.notes: TUI-only editor over in-repo config. Same question as config.write: is editing repo config a UI concern at all?

capabilities.title: Install / uninstall / restart / status the daemon
capabilities.notes: Both UIs are clients OF the daemon: the web is served BY it (a web button to stop the daemon would kill the page), and the TUI needs it running to render. Host-lifecycle control belongs to the CLI.

capabilities.title: Show / rotate the daemon bearer token
capabilities.notes: A credential-bootstrap verb: the web must already HAVE the token to talk to the daemon, so it cannot be where you go to get it. Rotating from the web would sever the rotating client.

capabilities.title: Diagnose setup and leaked resources
capabilities.notes: Diagnoses the host install, including the case where the daemon is down — exactly when neither UI can run.

capabilities.title: Upgrade af
capabilities.notes: Replaces the running binary; cannot be driven from a client the binary is serving.

capabilities.title: Factory-reset af
capabilities.notes: Destroys the state both UIs are rendering, daemon included. Owner-locked as a typed-confirmation CLI op (#1736).

capabilities.title: Bundle a bug report
capabilities.notes: Collects host-side logs and redacted state; must work when af is too broken to render a UI.

capabilities.title: Discover version / keys / API catalog / debug paths
capabilities.notes: af version/keys/debug/api are introspection verbs for humans and scripts at a shell. The TUI's ? covers the keys half for its own surface.

capabilities.title: Internal / non-user commands
capabilities.notes: af gen-docs is Hidden:true (CI docs generation). af agent-server is daemon-consumed plumbing — its own Long says it 'exists to be consumed by a daemon rather than opened by a person' — and af --daemon is a hidden flag. None are user capabilities.

capabilities.title: Move around the TUI (cursor, focus, sections, scroll, search, help, quit)

web.pointer: web/src/nav.ts:109-200 (its own j/k, [/], 1-9, Enter, Esc, Alt+j/k/w)

capabilities.notes: Navigation is per-surface chrome, not a product capability: each UI navigates its own layout idiomatically and a CLI has nothing to navigate. Deliberately NOT held to key-for-key parity — see docs/surface-parity.md.

capabilities.title: Open / split / hide / focus workspace panes
capabilities.notes: Both UIs have a pane workspace with surface-idiomatic gestures (the web adds mouse drag-and-drop the TUI cannot express; the TUI has keyboard splits the web reaches via Alt-chords). Layout is client-local and never hits the daemon.

_comment: Maps every DERIVED surface item to the capability it belongs to. The drift
check fails on any derived item missing here, which is what forces a new
verb/route/binding/RPC to get a parity decision at the moment it lands.
'-' means the item is not a user capability (grouping node, internal route).
The OPTION dimension: a surface can call the right verb while silently
declining half its request. That is where real gaps live — #1933 (the TUI
never sets CreateSessionRequest.Backend) and #1948 (the CLI never sets
Every field a surface does not send must be declared with exactly one of:
{"gap": "<capability-id>"}  - a real divergence, tracked by that capability
{"ok": "<reason>"}          - its absence is correct, and here is why
The field LISTS are derived from code, never hand-kept: wire structs by
reflection, the CLI/TUI by walking the AST of api/ and app/ for
daemon.XxxRequest composite literals, and the web from its call sites. So a
new field on any request forces a decision on every surface that builds it.
requests: keyed by daemon request TYPE -> surface (cli/tui) -> json field.
web_rpcs: keyed by RPC method -> json field.

title_base.ok: The CLI sends Title and errors on a duplicate rather than auto-suffixing; title_base is the UIs' friendly-collision path. See session.create.opt.title.

force_remote.ok: The CLI reaches the hook backend explicitly via --backend hook, which wins over the legacy ForceRemote selector (session/instance_factory.go:102-108). Setting both would be redundant.

tab_index.ok: The CLI addresses the tab by --name; tab_index is the ordinal fallback the daemon only consults when TabName is empty (daemon/control_types.go:248).
Same as the CLI: the TUI passes TabName, which is the daemon's preferred lookup key.

defer_while_attached.ok: A scheduler concern, not a user option: it holds a send while a TUI is attached full-screen (daemon/control_types.go:167). The daemon's own task delivery sets it; a human running `af sessions send-prompt --create` wants the prompt delivered now.

title.ok: The web sends title_base so the daemon auto-suffixes a collision and returns the resolved title (web/src/api.ts:236-238) — the TUI's friendly behavior. See session.create.opt.title.
